law.size: Exactly one font size on every surface; the headline is heavier and blacker, never larger.
law.registers: Three inks by three weights (400 / 550 / 800): nine registers.
law.accent: The accent is yours: define your scheme and use it with judgment. Identity comes from ink, hairlines, type, and motion — never from a color-counting rule. Color earns its place; it is not ambient.
law.status: Status rides the glyph: danger, success, and warning color the icon, never the sentence, never a filled pill.
law.layout: Every view fits the viewport. 100dvh screens, no page scrolling; a quiet nav swaps views when content exceeds one screen.
law.motion: Motion is feedback, never decoration: small, brief, eased, and gone under prefers-reduced-motion. State resolutions are gentler and resolve once; success persists, nothing changes size.
law.modes: Light and dark, defaulting to the system, with a user toggle. The change itself is one soft 700ms breath (view transition), uniform color ease where unsupported.
law.steering: Downstream projects steer the scheme: --ae-accent / --ae-accent-dark, and the status triplet when the domain needs different hues. Everything else is the shared identity.
law.buttons: Buttons are not links: contained ink shapes, solid primary and hairline secondary.

contrast.note: WCAG 2.1 ratios for every ink against every surface, recomputed whenever a color token changes. Contract: faint ink is decoration only — input placeholders supplement always-visible labels, and the resting link underline is decorative. Nothing interactive rests at faint. Status inks ride glyphs (3:1 non-text applies) but pass 4.5:1 anyway.
